---
title: "Creating Process Variables"
---

Process variables are used to store values and pass them between BPMN elements throughout a process instance. For example, values entered into a form as part of a user task can be sent to process variables in the process, which in turn can send those values to a decision table to evaluate and choose the direction the process should take. This topic describes how you can create process variables for the IDP connector.

For more general information on configuring process variables, see the corresponding sections in the Hyland Automate Modeler documentation: [Creating and Editing Process Variables](https://support.hyland.com/access?dita:topicPath=xto1673519414848.dita).

To create process variables for the IDP connector:

1.  On the Document AI Process page, click on the blank canvas and in the Property panel, click Edit Process Variables. The Process variables page is displayed.    ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  Click Create process variable. The Create variable page is displayed.
3.  Complete the following steps to create a content variable of variable type Array`<content>` to store content.
1. In the Variable Name field, type Content.
2. In the Variable type field, select Primitives > Array > Array`<content>`
3. Click Save and add new.
4.  Complete the following steps to create a BatchState variable of variable type json to include all the data like document classes and fields while documents move through the process workflow.
1. In the Variable Name field, type BatchState.
2. In the Variable type field, select Primitives > json
3. Click Save and add new.
5.  Complete the following steps to create a rejectReasons variable of type `Array<Any>`. to include rejection reasons used for Review and Verification screens.
1. In the Variable Name field, type rejectReasons.
2. In the Variable type field, select Primitives > Array > `Array<any>`
3. Under Default Value, select the Expression option and enter the following code.
```
[
  {
    "id": "1",
    "value": "Unknown document type"
  },
  {
    "id": "2",
    "value": "Document missing pages"
  },
  {
    "id": "3",
    "value": "Low quality scan (illegible)"
  }
]
```

4. Click Save.
![](data:image/gif;base64,)
